data.title: Stablecoins Resemble Fragmented FX Markets, Says Eco CEO
data.description: Stablecoins are exhibiting characteristics similar to fragmented foreign exchange markets, according to Eco CEO Ryne Saxe. According to NS3.AI, Saxe highlighted that the stablecoin sector, valued at over $320 billion, is experiencing liquidity fragmentation across various chains and pools. He noted that executing larger transfers often necessitates multi-route execution. Additionally, research by Borderless revealed that pricing discrepancies at the provider level within the same corridor can surpass hundreds of basis points.

data.title: Truss Criticizes Central Banking, Defends Mini-Budget
data.description: Former UK Prime Minister Liz Truss has voiced criticism of central banking practices while defending her controversial mini-budget. According to CoinDesk, Truss suggested that incorporating Bitcoin and discussions at the CPAC UK conference could serve as catalysts for economic reform. Truss's mini-budget, which faced significant backlash, aimed to stimulate economic growth through tax cuts and increased public spending. However, it was met with skepticism from financial markets and political opponents, leading to its eventual withdrawal. Truss argues that the current central banking system is flawed and that alternative financial systems, such as cryptocurrencies, could offer solutions to existing economic challenges. She believes that Bitcoin, with its decentralized nature, could play a role in reshaping financial systems and promoting economic stability. The CPAC UK conference, known for its focus on conservative policies, provides a platform for discussing such reforms. Truss's comments highlight ongoing debates about the role of traditional financial institutions and the potential of digital currencies in shaping future economic policies. Her stance reflects a growing interest in exploring innovative approaches to address economic issues, despite the controversies surrounding her previous fiscal policies.

data.title: XRP Price Debate Intensifies Amid Ripple's Stablecoin Volume Projection
data.description: The discussion surrounding XRP's potential price target of $50 has gained momentum following a social media post that connected Ripple's projected $33 trillion stablecoin volume to future demand for XRP. According to NS3.AI, this projection has sparked debates among investors and analysts about the cryptocurrency's future valuation. Geoffrey Kendrick from Standard Chartered has forecasted XRP to reach $8 by 2026 and $28 by 2030, adding further intrigue to the ongoing conversation.

data.title: Former UK Prime Minister Liz Truss Expresses Interest in Bitcoin Amid Economic Concerns
data.description: Former U.K. Prime Minister Liz Truss, who held office for 45 days in 2022, has expressed a keen interest in Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, Truss highlighted concerns about the U.K. economy, citing issues such as currency debasement, weak growth, regulation, taxation, and increasing state control as contributing to a "very negative trajectory."

data.title: Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister: The date for the next round of negotiations with the US cannot be determined until an agreement is reached.
data.description: According to Al Jazeera, when asked about a possible new round of negotiations with the United States, Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh stated that talks could not proceed until both sides reached an agreement on a common framework. "We cannot set a date for negotiations until we reach an agreement on a framework," Khatibzadeh said. He emphasized that anything Iran agrees to in the negotiations must uphold its rights under international law. Iran will not accept being an exception to international law, nor will it relinquish its rights. (Jin Shi)

data.title: XRP News: XRP Leads Major Crypto Gains With 8% Weekly Outperformance
data.description:
Key Takeaways
XRP is up around 8% over the past week, outperforming both Bitcoin and Ether.XRP has climbed back toward the key $1.43–$1.44 resistance zone.The token is trading above its 200-day EMA, improving the technical outlook.A confirmed breakout above $1.44 could open the path toward $1.60 and higher.
XRP Outperforms Bitcoin and Ether
XRP has emerged as one of the strongest-performing major cryptocurrencies over the past week, gaining around 8% while Bitcoin and Ether posted smaller advances.
The move has been steady rather than explosive, with XRP rising through a series of higher lows instead of sharp vertical spikes. That type of price action often suggests controlled accumulation rather than short-term speculation.
XRP Approaches Key Resistance Zone
XRP is now trading close to the important $1.43–$1.44 resistance area.
This zone has capped multiple rally attempts in recent months and remains the most important level for bulls to reclaim. A clean break above $1.44 would likely:
Confirm a short-term breakoutStrengthen momentumIncrease the probability of a move toward $1.60
Below that, the main support level to watch is around $1.40. As long as XRP remains above that area, near-term momentum remains constructive.
Technical Structure Is Improving
XRP recently moved back above its 200-day exponential moving average, which is often viewed as a sign of improving long-term trend strength.
Other constructive signals include:
A series of higher lowsImproving relative strength against Bitcoin and EtherOversold conditions on longer-term XRP/BTC charts beginning to reverse
However, traders are still watching for stronger volume confirmation. Without increased participation, XRP risks remaining trapped inside a broader consolidation range.
Why XRP’s Relative Strength Matters
When XRP starts outperforming Bitcoin and Ether, it often attracts attention because it can signal an early-stage rotation into altcoins.
Historically, XRP has tended to move later in the cycle but often does so aggressively once momentum builds. Analysts continue to point to a multi-year breakout structure forming on longer timeframes, though that thesis still requires confirmation through higher prices and stronger volume.
What Comes Next?
The near-term outlook depends on whether XRP can decisively clear $1.44.
Key levels to watch:
Resistance: $1.44Next upside target: $1.60Near-term support: $1.40Broader downside support: $1.30
A sustained breakout above $1.44 would strengthen the bullish case significantly, while another rejection could leave XRP range-bound for longer.

data.title: Israeli Military Engages Hezbollah Members Amid Ceasefire Violations
data.description:
On April 18, the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) spokesperson issued a statement regarding the situation during the ceasefire with Lebanon. According to BlockBeats, the IDF reported that several Hezbollah members were eliminated in southern Lebanon, south of the 'Yellow Line,' over the past 24 hours. These individuals were accused of violating the ceasefire agreement and posing an 'immediate threat' to Israeli forces.

The statement detailed that artillery was used to support ground troops in the region. In response to the perceived threats, the IDF destroyed parts of Hezbollah's infrastructure. The spokesperson emphasized that the military actions were conducted under directives from the political leadership. The IDF has been authorized to take necessary measures for self-defense, and efforts to neutralize threats are not restricted by the ceasefire agreement.

data.title: Global Markets Rebound Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
Global markets experienced a significant rebound over the past week, driven by expectations of easing tensions in the Middle East. According to BlockBeats, Iran's temporary announcement to open the Strait of Hormuz led to a rapid decline in oil prices, strengthening risk assets. U.S. stocks reached new highs, the dollar weakened, and gold approached the $4,900 mark. However, Iran later signaled continued military control, coupled with the U.S. maintaining its blockade, raising concerns about the fluctuating situation.

On a macro level, the progress of U.S.-Iran negotiations remains the biggest variable for the coming week. U.S. President Donald Trump indicated that talks might advance over the weekend, warning that if no agreement is reached by next Wednesday, the ceasefire could end, and conflict might resume. Iran remains cautious in negotiations, particularly on key issues like uranium enrichment, where significant differences persist. The market has shifted from pricing in conflict escalation to a path of easing, but any sudden changes could still cause significant asset volatility.

Regarding interest rate expectations, the decline in energy prices has eased inflationary pressures, with market expectations for a Federal Reserve rate cut this year rising to about 60%. Meanwhile, Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Warsh is set to attend a Senate hearing next week, where his policy stance, especially whether it leans dovish, will be a crucial factor influencing gold and risk assets.

Key economic data releases include the U.S. March retail sales monthly rate on Tuesday at 20:30, initial jobless claims for the week ending April 18 on Thursday at 20:30, and the preliminary April S&P Global Manufacturing/Services PMI on Thursday at 21:45. On Friday at 22:00, the final April University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index and one-year inflation rate expectations will be released. In the short term, the market will focus on three main variables: U.S.-Iran negotiation progress, oil price trends, and Federal Reserve policy signals.

data.title: Bitcoin News Today: Strategy Proposes Semi-Monthly Dividends for STRC Preferred Stock
data.description:
Key Takeaways
Strategy wants to shift STRC preferred stock dividends from monthly to semi-monthly payments.The change would not affect STRC’s 11.5% annual dividend yield.Management says the goal is to reduce volatility, improve liquidity, and increase investor demand.If approved, the first semi-monthly dividend payment would be made on July 15.
Strategy Looks to Increase Dividend Frequency
Strategy has filed a proposal that would allow its STRC “Stretch” preferred stock to pay dividends twice per month instead of once per month.
The company said the move is designed to make the preferred shares more stable and attractive to investors without changing the total annual payout.
Dividend Yield Remains Unchanged
The proposed adjustment would not affect STRC’s current annual dividend rate of 11.5%.
Instead, shareholders would simply receive smaller payments more frequently.
According to Executive Chairman Michael Saylor, the change is intended to:
Stabilize the stock priceReduce cyclical trading swingsImprove liquidityIncrease investor demand
STRC Has Become One of Strategy’s Most Popular Products
STRC has quickly become one of Strategy’s most successful financing vehicles.
Outstanding value in the preferred stock has grown to approximately $6.4 billion, reflecting strong investor demand for high-yield exposure tied to Bitcoin-related assets.
The company noted that volatility in STRC has already declined significantly:
Average volatility was around 13% during the first eight months after launchVolatility has since fallen to roughly 2.1% over the last two months
Management believes semi-monthly dividend payments could reduce volatility even further.
Key Dates to Watch
Shareholders are expected to vote on the proposal by June 8.
If approved:
The first semi-monthly payment would be made on July 15Future dividends would likely be distributed every two weeks
Strategy Shares Rise Alongside Bitcoin
Strategy shares climbed nearly 12% as Bitcoin rallied back toward $77,000.
The strong performance reflects continued investor enthusiasm for Bitcoin-related equities as crypto prices recover.

data.title: RAVE briefly fell below 18 USDT, dropping 24.17% in one hour.
data.description: OKX data shows that RAVE briefly fell below 18 USDT and is currently trading at 18.76 USDT, a drop of 24.17% in one hour.

data.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's Leadership in Question Amid IPO Concerns
data.description:
According to Foresight News, some shareholders have expressed doubts about OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's ability to lead an initial public offering (IPO). They are considering board chairman Bret Taylor as a potential replacement. However, Bret Taylor and the majority of investors continue to support Altman.

Previously, Altman sought OpenAI's involvement in a new round of funding for fusion startup Helion, which has faced delays in its energy breakthrough and is experiencing financial strain. Additionally, he pursued OpenAI's support for rocket manufacturer Stoke Space. These investments are not part of OpenAI's core business. Recently, the company informed employees of the need to cut non-core projects to focus on increasing competitive pressures.

data.title: Iranian Forces Engage Indian Vessels in Hormuz Strait Incident
data.description: On April 18, two Indian vessels attempting to navigate the Hormuz Strait were confronted by the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Navy, resulting in gunfire. According to BlockBeats, one of the ships involved was a supertanker flying the Indian flag. Iran has yet to issue a statement regarding the incident.

data.title: Moonbags, Bitcoin, and The Trifecta: Wendy O Unveils Her 4-Tier Strategy and AI Predictions
data.description:
In a recent episode of the Inside the Blockchain 100 AMA hosted by Binance Square, prominent crypto influencer Wendy O opened up about her remarkable journey from an underpaid healthcare worker to one of the most recognized female voices in the cryptocurrency space.
Boasting over a million combined followers across X, YouTube, and TikTok, Wendy O, who has been navigating the volatile crypto markets since 2017, shared her hard-won trading strategies, her bullish outlook on the intersection of AI and Web3, and why she believes crypto is the ultimate equalizer for women in finance.
From Long Commutes to Crypto Independence
Before she was a household name in crypto content, Wendy O was a patient care coordinator in Los Angeles. Despite loving her job, she felt stifled by the grueling commutes and the lack of upward financial mobility in the healthcare sector.
"There's not a lot of opportunity to get raises," Wendy O explained to host Karine. Seeking a way to be a stay-at-home mom for her young daughter, she began searching for alternatives. She discovered Bitcoin through libertarian radio broadcasts, which sparked her curiosity. Soon after, on the advice of a family friend, she hesitantly joined social media, eventually hosting over four dozen local crypto meetups across LA County.
Today, she credits the decentralized nature of the industry for changing her life. "The cool thing about crypto is it's 24/7, 365. All you really need is a cell phone to access it. It’s been a game changer for me."
The Maturation of a Trader: The 4-Tier Strategy
Looking back at her early days in 2017—when trading was heavily reliant on Bitcoin pairs rather than stablecoins—Wendy O admits to making rookie mistakes and engaging in risky trading behaviors. Today, her approach has dramatically matured, shifting from aggressive day trading to patient, long-term swing trading.
"The market is going to do what the market's going to do whether you like it or not. The market doesn't care about your feelings," she warned.
To manage risk, Wendy O now utilizes a strict four-tier investment system:
Tier 1: Bitcoin. Treated as a pristine collateral asset. "That’s something I don’t plan on selling ever," she noted, adding that she securely stores it off-site via financial advisors.Tier 2: Long-term Holds. Established altcoins like Ethereum, Solana, and XRP, mapped out with specific profit-taking targets.Tier 3: New Projects. High-potential new launches where she gets in and out rapidly.Tier 4: Meme Coins. Highly speculative assets where she previously applied the "moonbag strategy"—pulling out the initial investment and a small profit as soon as the asset pumps, leaving the "free" tokens to ride the market.
Her number one rule for surviving the crypto space? Keep a journal. Wendy O stressed that self-accountability is paramount. Traders must meticulously document their investments and profit-taking to avoid the ultimate trap: marrying your bags and blowing up your account.
The Future is AI and 'The Trifecta'
Looking toward the remainder of 2024 and into 2026, Wendy O is placing her bets heavily on the integration of Artificial Intelligence and cryptocurrency.
"I think that AI agents are going to take over in their own way, and it’s going to be a big part of crypto just because of microtransactions," she stated. For investors looking for the next narrative, she highlighted projects featuring what she calls "The Trifecta": AI, DeFi, and RWAs (Real World Assets).
Ultimately, Wendy O envisions a future where blockchain technology is entirely frictionless. "Everything’s going to be on-chain... future generations are going to be operating in crypto, and they're not even going to know they're operating in crypto."
A Champion for Women in Web3
Throughout her career, Wendy O has been a vocal advocate for female participation in the blockchain sector. While acknowledging that the internet can be a "weird place," she firmly believes the crypto industry has progressed and remains unparalleled in its accessibility.
"There are no real barriers to entry," she said. "If a woman wants to learn about crypto... we have so many capabilities at our fingertips. I actually think that crypto is the best place for a woman to work."
For those looking to follow in her footsteps and become crypto content creators, Wendy O’s advice is characteristically blunt and practical: "Consistency is incredibly important... get your behind up and get to work." She added that if she had to start all over again today, she would aggressively create content across multiple platforms, lean on AI for assistance, and most importantly: "I would not worry about what people thought about me, and I wouldn't read the comments."
The full interview with Wendy O is available on Binance Square's '[Inside the Blockchain 100](https://www.binance.com/en/square/audio/replay?id=39008457541242)'.

data.title: Sources: Strait of Hormuz closed again
data.description: Shipping sources say merchant ships have received radio messages from the Iranian Navy informing them that the Strait of Hormuz is closed again, prohibiting any vessels from passing through. (Jinshi)

data.title: Banking Groups Intensify Lobbying Against Stablecoin Yield Ban in CLARITY Act Talks
data.description: Banking trade groups have increased their lobbying efforts against the proposed stablecoin yield compromise in ongoing CLARITY Act discussions. According to NS3.AI, the debate has focused on a White House estimate suggesting that a yield ban would result in only $2.1 billion in additional bank lending. The compromise aims to prohibit passive yield on stablecoin balances while permitting activity-based rewards. Senator Tillis mentioned that the text is still being finalized this week, whereas Senator Alsobrooks anticipates its completion probably next week.

data.title: Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister: US blockade of Iranian ports "will never be effective"
data.description: Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh stated that Iran will never accept a US blockade of its ports or any other "orders" issued by the US. "There will never be any blockades in the future," he said. "The era of colonialism must end," he added. The US "cannot dictate to other countries." (Jinshi)

data.title: Several ships attempted to pass through the Strait of Hormuz but ultimately turned back.
data.description: Ship tracking data shows that approximately 10 vessels attempted to pass through the Strait of Hormuz on the 18th but ultimately turned back. Shipping tracks indicate that several vessels turned back after reaching waters near Iran's Larak Island. Data shows that most of these vessels were oil tankers, and many were destined for India according to the Automatic Identification System (AIS). Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi announced on the 17th that the Strait of Hormuz would be open to all merchant ships during the ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel. However, Iranian media reported on the 18th that due to the United States' "repeated breaches of promises," the Iranian military had tightened control over the Strait of Hormuz again. (Xinhua)

data.title: Ethereum Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: Ethereum's price fluctuations may lead to substantial liquidations on major centralized exchanges. According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass indicates that if Ethereum surpasses $2,473, the cumulative liquidation intensity of short positions on mainstream centralized exchanges could reach $1.222 billion. Conversely, if Ethereum falls below $2,244, the cumulative liquidation intensity of long positions could amount to $712 million.
